message:
fix(harness): use deterministic exit(134) for crash row + bump matrix isolation timeout

Two related stabilizations for the matrix isolation tests on hosts where
the sysctl/kernel core-dump pipeline is slow.

bin/harness.rs
- The synthetic __harness_test_crash function previously called
  std::process::abort(), which routes through abort(3) and the registered
  SIGABRT handler. On hosts that pipe core dumps through systemd-coredump
  or apport, the kernel can hold the dying process long enough that the
  parent's case timeout (the thing this fixture is supposed to be
  *opposite* of) wins the race. The matrix would then misclassify a
  crash as a timeout. Replace abort() with std::process::exit(134), which
  reproduces the conventional 128+SIGABRT exit status without taking the
  core-dump path. The matrix only inspects the exit status, so the crash
  classification is identical, and we no longer fight the host's
  coredump policy.

tests/conformance_matrix_test.rs
- Bump --case-timeout-ms from 100 to 1000 for the
  isolated_case_runner_surfaces_timeout_and_crash_rows test. 100ms is
  too aggressive on busy CI hosts; the 30s sleep in the timeout fixture
  still triggers at 1s, but the crash fixture's exit(134) now reliably
  finishes inside the window even when the runner has cold caches.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
